---
title: OpenOrganelle
alias: &ALIAS openorganelle
href: https://openorganelle.janelia.org/
listing:
  id: pubs
  template: ../../../../_ejs/publications-people.ejs 
  contents: 
    - "../../../../publications/**/*.qmd"
    - "!../../../../publications/_template/"
  sort: "pub_number desc"
  include:
    categories: *ALIAS
  fields: [publication, title, categories, image, date, author]

---

## Overview

OpenOrganelle (Janelia Research Campus) provides large-scale electron microscopy volumes and segmentations of cells at nanometer resolution, enabling 3D analysis of subcellular structures.

## Key Features

- Hosts FIB-SEM volumes with isotropic nanometer resolution across multiple cell types
- Provides 3D segmentations of organelles including mitochondria, ER, nucleus, and lysosomes
- Uses cloud-native N5/Zarr formats for scalable access to multi-terabyte datasets
- Enables interactive 3D visualization via Neuroglancer and companion analysis tools
- Open data with metadata describing cell type, organism, and imaging conditions

## Use Cases in Research

- Studying subcellular structural changes in host cells during bacterial or viral infection
- Analyzing organelle morphology and dynamics relevant to intracellular pathogen survival
- Training AI segmentation models for automated organelle detection in EM images
- Investigating membrane ultrastructure changes associated with antibiotic-induced cell stress
- Integrating structural data with transcriptomic or proteomic data for subcellular systems analysis

## Getting Started

Visit [OpenOrganelle](https://openorganelle.janelia.org/) to access the database.

## Related Publications

:::{#pubs}
:::
